name: Connecting the dots — verified links across filings, deals and time
note: Each connection is an edge between facts ALREADY published elsewhere on this dashboard — no figure here is new. connectionTier states the link's confidence: both_sides (both parties on record), one_side (only one party names it), inference (analyst framing over sourced facts). This week nothing clears the both_sides bar — an honesty signal, not a gap. Curated by hand; the daily news refresh only proposes candidates.

items.title: CoreWeave names Meta as a committed customer — Meta's own filings name no provider
items.insight: CoreWeave's ~$99B backlog disclosure lists Meta among its committed customers. Meta separately discloses $182.9B of not-yet-commenced leases and $237.7B of purchase commitments — but names no counterparty. Only the seller's filing reveals the relationship, so how much of Meta's book is CoreWeave capacity stays undisclosed. A search of Meta's filings alone would never surface this link.
items.namedBy: Seller only — CoreWeave names Meta; Meta does not name CoreWeave.

evidence.label: CoreWeave ~$99B backlog tied to commitments from Nvidia, Meta, Microsoft, OpenAI

items.title: Microsoft's unopened lease book, the Pecos build and the Mount Pleasant opening are one motion
items.insight: $196.6B of not-yet-commenced leases is paper capacity; the 2 GW Pecos campus is that paper turning into steel; the $3.3B Mount Pleasant opening (Jul 2) is it turning into live load. Read as a sequence — contract, build, commission — three separate press releases become one strategy: converting the largest unopened lease book in the sector into operating megawatts.

evidence.label: Microsoft: $196.6B not-yet-commenced leases — the largest disclosed book
Pecos 2 GW campus + Chevron 2.67 GW behind-the-meter supply
Mount Pleasant $3.3B data center opened Jul 2

items.title: Oracle's filed backlog, the $300B OpenAI contract and the Abilene builds are one deal seen three ways
items.insight: Oracle carries $260B of not-yet-commenced leases on ~$32B of annual operating cash flow — roughly a decade pre-committed. The reported $300B OpenAI Stargate contract is the customer side of that obligation; the Abilene ledger records (1.2 GW under construction) are the concrete. But even here the book is being trimmed — the 600 MW Abilene expansion is cancelled — so the filed backlog slightly overstates what will physically land.
items.namedBy: Oracle side filed and reported; OpenAI is private and does not file.

evidence.label: Oracle: $260B not-yet-commenced leases on ~$32B OCF (~10 yrs pre-committed)
$300B OpenAI Stargate contract — Oracle the largest single holder
Abilene: 1.2 GW under construction; 600 MW expansion cancelled

items.title: Nvidia funds the buildout it also supplies
items.insight: Nvidia shows up as investor or backer AND chip vendor to the same counterparties — the IREN 5 GW partnership, CoreWeave's backlog (Nvidia named among committers), and KKR's Helix vehicle (Nvidia a co-investor). Equity and partnership dollars flow out; GPU purchase orders flow back. That loop is what the counterparty map on Capital traces — and why part of the 'demand' for Nvidia chips is demand Nvidia itself helped finance.

evidence.label: Nvidia + IREN: up to 5 GW partnership, Sweetwater flagship (DSX design)
CoreWeave ~$99B backlog names Nvidia among committers
KKR Helix: $10B+ committed with Nvidia, Vistra, KIA

items.title: Microsoft is shelving sites while its lease book climbs — pruning and accelerating at once
items.insight: Microsoft paused its Licking County, Ohio campus (now in the Graveyard) in the same window its not-yet-commenced lease book grew ~$41B to $196.6B. Not an error to reconcile — a portfolio reshuffle: cutting speculative sites while committing harder where power and demand line up. The signal for everyone else: even the most aggressive builder is culling.
